question: Did the model output Option D or the value 2√3 (or equivalent numerical value approx 3.46) as the final answer?
Did the model correctly deduce from the three views that the tetrahedron is embedded in a cube (or bounding box) with an edge length of 2?
Did the model correctly interpret the geometric features of the views (Front/Top as right triangles, Side as a square with a diagonal)?
Did the model correctly identify that three of the tetrahedron's faces are right-angled triangles?
Did the model correctly identify the fourth (slanted) face as an equilateral triangle?
Did the model correctly calculate the length of the face diagonals (the sides of the equilateral face) as 2√2 (or √8)?
Did the model correctly calculate the area of the right-angled face with legs 2 and 2 as 2?
Did the model correctly calculate the area of the right-angled faces with legs 2 and 2√2 as 2√2?
Did the model correctly calculate the area of the equilateral face as 2√3 using the appropriate formula?
Did the model correctly compare the calculated areas (2, 2√2, 2√3) to determine that 2√3 is the maximum?
Did the model generate a 3D geometric reconstruction image of the tetrahedron (or the tetrahedron embedded in a cube) to assist in reasoning?
In the generated 3D image, are the vertices of the tetrahedron clearly labeled and legible?
If the model generated an image of the cube with the tetrahedron, are the solid and dashed lines used appropriately to indicate visible and hidden edges?
Does the generated 3D image correctly depict the tetrahedron embedded within a cube (e.g., vertices positioned at correct corners relative to the described views)?
Do the vertex labels used in the textual reasoning match the labels present in the generated image(s)?
If the text describes a specific face as a right-angled or equilateral triangle, does the generated image visually correspond to this description (no obvious contradictions)?
Do the edge lengths or relationships (e.g., face diagonals) mentioned in the text match what is depicted or labeled in the generated images?
